name: Capterra: Product Details + Reviews
summary: Fetch product metadata and reviews for a Capterra product. Reads the embedded JSON-LD SoftwareApplication block instead of scraping DOM (more stable, returns ~25 reviews).
description: Open a Capterra product page and return product metadata plus reviews from the embedded JSON-LD SoftwareApplication block. JSON-LD is much more reliable than DOM scraping on Capterra (Cloudflare/SPA); typical pages return ~25 reviews per call.

product_url.description: Full Capterra product URL — e.g. the .../p/<id>/<slug>/reviews/ canonical from capterra/search.

inputs.script: const norm = (v) => { const t = String(v || '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim(); return t || 'N/A'; }; const scripts = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll("script[type='application/ld+json']")); let app = null; let breadcrumb = null; for (const s of scripts) { try { const data = JSON.parse(s.textContent); const candidates = Array.isArray(data) ? data : [data]; for (const c of candidates) { if (c && c['@type'] === 'SoftwareApplication') app = c; if (c && c['@type'] === 'BreadcrumbList') breadcrumb = c; } } catch (_) {} } if (!app) throw new Error('No SoftwareApplication JSON-LD block found on this page'); const agg = app.aggregateRating || {}; const category = (() => { if (!breadcrumb || !Array.isArray(breadcrumb.itemListElement)) return 'N/A'; const items = breadcrumb.itemListElement.filter((i) => i && i.name).map((i) => i.name); return items.length ? items.join(' > ') : 'N/A'; })(); const product = { name: norm(app.name), description: norm(app.description), category, rating: agg.ratingValue != null ? String(agg.ratingValue) : 'N/A', total_reviews: agg.reviewCount != null ? String(agg.reviewCount) : 'N/A', best_rating: agg.bestRating != null ? String(agg.bestRating) : '5', url: location.href.replace(/[?#].*$/, '') }; const rawReviews = Array.isArray(app.review) ? app.review : []; const reviews = rawReviews.map((r) => { const rating = r && r.reviewRating ? r.reviewRating : {}; return { author: norm(r && r.author && r.author.name), body: norm(r && r.reviewBody), rating: rating.ratingValue != null ? String(rating.ratingValue) : 'N/A', best_rating: rating.bestRating != null ? String(rating.bestRating) : '5' }; }); return { product, reviews, reviews_returned: reviews.length };

help.summary: Fetch product metadata and reviews for a Capterra product. Reads the embedded JSON-LD SoftwareApplication block instead of scraping DOM (more stable, returns ~25 reviews).

parameters.product_url: Full Capterra product URL — e.g. the .../p/<id>/<slug>/reviews/ canonical from capterra/search.

returns: Object: { product: { name, description, category, rating, total_reviews, best_rating, url }, reviews: [{ author, body, rating, best_rating }], reviews_returned }. Capterra exposes ~25 reviews per page in its JSON-LD; for deeper coverage walk additional ?page=N URLs separately.
notes: Capterra is fronted by Cloudflare. Cold sessions may hit a 'Just a moment...' challenge — open the URL once in the same browser profile to clear it before re-running.

Source of truth is script[type='application/ld+json'] containing @type='SoftwareApplication'. The DOM-scraping approach in v1 returned zero reviews because Capterra removed [itemprop='review'] markup.
JSON-LD only ships ~25 reviews per page. Pagination via ?page=N would need a follow-up workflow if deep coverage is required.
